query: Terrion Arnold Detroit Lions Commissioner's Exempt List released waived NFL transaction status July 2026
answer:
Research complete. Here's the summary:

**Terrion Arnold — Status as of July 7, 2026:**

- **Waived/released by the Detroit Lions on June 29, 2026**, the same day he posted a $1 million bond, following his June 24, 2026 arrest on 8 felony counts (4 kidnapping, 4 armed robbery) tied to an alleged February 2026 incident near Tampa.
- **Cleared waivers UNCLAIMED on Monday, July 6, 2026**, making him an **unrestricted free agent** eligible to sign with any team.
- **NOT on the Commissioner's Exempt List** — this is being discussed as a possibility publicly (including speculation about whether Goodell will act), but no such action has been taken. Since he's now an unsigned free agent, the Exempt List question is moot unless a team signs him.
- At least **3 NFL teams** reportedly contacted his representatives; his attorney anticipates a signing within 30 days (though unconfirmed).

Facts were cross-validated across Detroit News, Pro Football Rumors, On3, Pride of Detroit, Yardbarker, and NBC Sports.

sources.title: Former Lion Terrion Arnold clears waivers, eligible to sign elsewhere
sources.snippet: Arnold cleared waivers Monday, making him a free agent. He was released by the Lions on June 29 after he was arrested in connection to an alleged armed robbery and kidnapping.

sources.title: Lions Waive CB Terrion Arnold
sources.snippet: The Lions have waived cornerback Terrion Arnold, per a team announcement. The former first-round pick was recently charged with eight felonies in Florida.

sources.title: Terrion Arnold passes through waivers unclaimed following Lions release, arrest
sources.snippet: Arnold passed through waivers unclaimed after the Lions released him; he is now an unrestricted free agent. Attorney anticipates he signs within 30 days.

sources.title: Former Lions CB Terrion Arnold clears waivers despite interest from teams
sources.snippet: Arnold cleared waivers Monday. It's possible he ends up on the Commissioner's Exempt List but that has not occurred. Three NFL teams reached out.

sources.title: Reports: Former Lions CB Terrion Arnold clears waivers
sources.snippet: Arnold was arrested on June 24 and cleared waivers Monday; expected to meet with interested teams.

sources.title: Lions' decision to release Terrion Arnold has significant financial ramifications
sources.snippet: After being released on $1 million bond and cleared to practice, the Lions cut him; financial ramifications discussed.

sources.title: Terrion Arnold Drawing Interest; 3 Teams Could Sign Him
sources.snippet: Arnold no longer a member of the Lions; 2024 24th-overall pick out of Alabama.

data_points.metric: NFL teams reportedly interested after clearing waivers
